Hard-reload the browser with Shift + F5 on Windows or Cmd + Shift + R on Mac. If that doesn’t help, fully close all browser windows and reopen. Allow local-network access in your browser. Edge and Chrome have a permission gate; if rapidreconciler.getgsi.com isn’t allowed, all data calls silently fail. Edge: Settings → Privacy, search, and services → Site permissions → All permissions → Local network access → ensure the URL is in “Allowed.” Chrome has the equivalent under its site-permissions area. Clear cookies and cached files for rapidreconciler.getgsi.com only (your IT can help). Close the browser fully, reopen, log in fresh. Check with your IT for recent network or security changes — new web filter, DNS filter, secure web gateway, endpoint security policy, Windows update, VPN config change. If multiple users at your site are affected at once, this is the most likely cause. For the full step-by-step runbook including IT-conversation hints (specific products to ask about, the cross-network test, what to whitelist), see the no-data-after-login scenario . ! Still blank after working through Steps 1–5? See Email RR Support .

Menu items are missing You can log in but the left-side navigation is missing items you used to see, or items you expected based on your role. This is a permissions issue, not a bug RapidReconciler shows different menu items to different users based on the permissions granted by your RR Administrator — the person at your company who manages user accounts. If you’re missing a menu item, it’s almost always because that permission isn’t set on your account yet. What to do Identify your RR Administrator — usually someone in finance or IT who handles user accounts in RapidReconciler. Tell them which menu item you’re missing and what you’re trying to do (e.g., “I need to see the In Transit module to reconcile transfer orders”). Your Administrator can adjust your permissions in Admin → Users ; the change is immediate. After they confirm, refresh the company-selector widget on your page or log out and back in for the new menu items to appear. i If your RR Administrator says the permissions are correct but you still don’t see the menu items, your browser session may be holding a cached older permission set. Log out completely and log back in. See the newly-granted-companies scenario for a fuller walkthrough.

Email RR Support If the steps in the topics above didn’t resolve your issue, email rrsupport@getgsi.com . The more context you include, the faster we can help. What to include What you’re seeing — the exact error message wording, or what the screen shows. A screenshot is ideal. What you tried — which steps from this page or a linked scenario you walked, and what happened on each. When it started — today only, since a recent change, etc. Scope — is it just you, multiple users at your company, or just one machine? For the “blank screen” case, this is the highest-value clue we can get. Anything that changed recently — new corporate web filter, VPN change, browser update, password rotation, etc. Email template To: rrsupport@getgsi.com Subject: RapidReconciler — [short symptom, e.g. "blank screen after login"] Hi RR Support, I’m hitting [describe what you’re seeing]. Started: [when] Affecting: [just me / multiple users / specific machine] I’ve tried: - [step 1, result] - [step 2, result] Recent changes I’m aware of: [if any] Screenshots attached. Thanks, NAME · CUSTOMER NAME

Period Close Troubleshooting Period-end is the highest-stakes window in the reconciliation cycle. When something doesn’t complete the way it should, you need a fast answer. Pick the symptom that matches what you’re seeing — most cases resolve before you need to email support. i RapidReconciler is read-only by design. It diagnoses variances and surfaces them on the dashboard; corrections happen in JDE. If the close won’t complete or a balance won’t clear, the fix almost always lives upstream in JDE (post a batch, correct a transaction, post a balancing JE) — the topics below tell you where in JDE to act. Pick your symptom If you’re seeing… Go to The period won’t close, the close button is greyed out, you can’t move to the new period, or the new period isn’t available in the dropdown Period won’t close The Inventory Validation indicator is red, you see “out of balance” or a variance message, the carry-forward shows an unreconciled amount Validation light is red The audit report won’t generate, the audit export comes through empty, or the audit run errors out Audit report won’t generate The new period’s opening balances don’t match the prior period’s closing — the carry-forward looks wrong on day 1 Carry-forward looks wrong None of the above, or the steps in a topic above didn’t resolve it Email RR Support Quick Reference Most common cause of period-close failure Unposted GL batches in JDE for the prior period Where to check the period’s health Inventory Validation indicator at the top of the Reconciliation page (green = clean roll-forward; red = variance) If you see a Cardex variance after month-end Email rrsupport@getgsi.com with the period, account, and a screenshot of the variance — this pattern needs hands-on support Support email rrsupport@getgsi.com Related guides For the full inventory reconciliation walkthrough including period-end checklists, see Inventory Reconciliation For PO Receipts period-end reconciliation, see PO Receipts Reconciliation For audit-report export issues that aren’t period-specific, see Reports & Exports Troubleshooting

Period won’t close The period won’t close cleanly, the close action is greyed out, you can’t advance to the new period, or the new period isn’t available in the period selector dropdown. RapidReconciler treats period close as gated on data being clean — which means the blocker is almost always something upstream that needs to be addressed before close can complete. The most common cause: unposted batches in JDE The reconciliation engine relies on the prior period’s GL batches being fully posted in JDE before it can roll forward. If any GL batch for the closing period is still unposted, the period close will fail or be blocked. In JDE, look for any unposted GL batches in the period you’re trying to close. Post them. Wait for the next RapidReconciler refresh (or trigger one manually if your role allows), then return and try the close again. If batches are all posted but close still fails Check the Inventory Validation indicator at the top of the Reconciliation page. If it’s red, the roll-forward has a variance — resolve that first. See Validation light is red . Check the System Status indicator . If that’s red, a back-end job has crashed and close won’t complete until the job recovers. See the system-status-red scenario . Confirm you’re looking at the right period. The Period End Selector at the top right of the module status bar drives what you see — make sure it’s set to the period you’re closing. Confirm your permissions . Some close actions are restricted to specific roles. If you used to be able to close periods and can’t now, your RR Administrator may have changed your permissions. If the new period isn’t in the dropdown If you can close the prior period but the next period isn’t showing up as a selectable option, this usually points at a configuration or data issue your administrator or RR support needs to look at — the period-end roll didn’t complete cleanly. Email rrsupport@getgsi.com with the company, account, and the period you expected to see. ! If batches are posted, status lights are green, and the close still won’t complete, see Email RR Support .

Validation light is red The Inventory Validation indicator at the top of the Reconciliation page has turned red, often paired with an “out of balance” or variance message. The carry-forward calculation panel typically shows an unreconciled amount. What this means The roll-forward from the prior period failed to balance. RapidReconciler is telling you that the closing position in the prior period and the opening position in the current period don’t reconcile — usually because something happened between them that the system can’t account for. The first thing to check (resolves ~80% of cases) Hover your cursor over the red indicator. The product’s own tooltip will tell you the most common cause: unposted GL batches in the prior period . In JDE, check whether any GL batches for the prior period are still unposted. If you find unposted batches, post them. Refresh the Reconciliation page in RapidReconciler. The indicator should go green — the carry-forward will recompute against the now-posted data. If all prior-period batches are confirmed posted Click the red indicator to export the variance Excel report. This is the artifact RR support needs to investigate transaction-level activity. See the inventory-validation-red scenario for the full runbook including what to attach. If the red light is paired with other symptoms If you’re also seeing a red System Status light, see the System status light is red scenario — that runbook covers the IT-side environment checks (disk, TempDB, SQL Agent, SSIS) that often clear paired symptoms. ! If all batches are posted and the variance Excel doesn’t reveal an obvious culprit, see Email RR Support with the Excel attached.

Audit report won’t generate The audit report is your sign-off artifact for period close. If it won’t generate, exports empty, or errors out, you can’t close cleanly until it produces a valid output. Try these steps in order Check the System Status light first. If it’s red, the back-end job has crashed and the audit report can’t complete until the job recovers. See the system-status-red scenario . Confirm the period selector at the top right is set to the period you’re trying to audit, not the new period or a different one. Hard-reload the page with Shift + F5 and try again — clears any cached state from a previous run that might be holding the audit in a broken intermediate state. If the audit downloads but is empty, apply the same checks as a regular export: filters, period selector, permission scope. See the “Export is empty / wrong” topic in Reports & Exports Troubleshooting . If the audit downloads but figures look wrong, check the Inventory Validation indicator. A red indicator means the period had a variance — the audit is reflecting that, not generating a new error. Resolve the validation issue first (see Validation light is red ). Try a smaller scope. Run the audit against a single business unit or company to see if the issue is data-volume or report-wide. If the audit consistently won’t complete If the audit is reproducibly failing for everyone at your site and status lights are green, the problem is likely on the GSI side — the audit job has stalled, or the underlying SQL Agent job that prepares audit data has stopped. See the stale-data scenario . ! If steps above don’t resolve it, see Email RR Support with the report name, the period, the filters, and a screenshot of the failure.

section_title: Carry-forward looks wrong on day 1 of the new period
body: Carry-forward looks wrong on day 1 of the new period You opened the new period and the opening balances don’t match the prior period’s closing balances. The carry-forward is showing wrong figures, ghost transactions, or unexpected variance from day 1. What this usually means One of three things: The prior period closed with a variance that was carried forward as residual. Check the prior period’s Inventory Validation indicator — if it was red at close, it carried that variance forward. A late transaction landed in the prior period after close, and the system is showing that delta as the difference between closing and opening. Check JDE for any backdated transactions in the prior period. The new period’s data hasn’t fully refreshed yet. RapidReconciler imports from JDE on a schedule. If you’re looking at the new period within minutes of close, the import may not have caught up yet. What to check Look at the prior period’s Validation indicator — if it’s red, the prior period’s variance is the source. Compare the prior period’s closing balance and the new period’s opening balance side by side. The difference itself often tells you what happened (e.g., a single transaction landing late). Check JDE for any backdated activity in the prior period after the close timestamp. Wait for the next refresh cycle if you just closed. Give it the customary refresh interval (usually 24 hours, but ask your RR Administrator for your specific cadence) before treating it as a real discrepancy. Hard-reload the page with Shift + F5 — sometimes the carry-forward UI is showing a stale calculation. If figures still don’t reconcile after a refresh Click the red Inventory Validation indicator on the new period (if it’s red) to export the variance Excel. The Excel will identify which specific transactions are causing the carry-forward mismatch, which is what RR support needs to diagnose further. If the symptoms include a red System Status light alongside the carry-forward variance, see the System status light is red scenario — the IT-side environment checks (disk, TempDB, SQL Agent, SSIS) often resolve the paired condition. ! If you’ve checked all of the above and the carry-forward still doesn’t reconcile, see Email RR Support .

Email RR Support Period-close issues are time-sensitive. The more context you include up front, the less back-and-forth before we can help. Email rrsupport@getgsi.com . What to include The period you’re trying to close, and the period selector value when the issue happens. What you’re seeing — the close button greyed out, an error, a red status light, missing dropdown option, etc. Screenshots ideal. The status of both indicators — Inventory Validation (green / red) and System Status (green / red). Whether the GL batches in the prior period are confirmed posted in JDE. The variance Excel if a Validation light or System Status light is red. Whether other users at your site are affected or it’s just you. The variance amount (if applicable) and which periods it spans. Email template To: rrsupport@getgsi.com Subject: RapidReconciler — Period close issue [period e.g. April 2026] Hi RR Support, I’m hitting [describe what you’re seeing] when trying to close [period]. Period: [period name] Inventory Validation light: [green / red] System Status light: [green / red] Variance amount (if applicable): [$X] Affecting: [just me / multiple users] Prior-period GL batches in JDE: [all posted / unposted batches found] I’ve tried: - [step 1, result] - [step 2, result] Attaching: - Variance Excel (if either status light is red) - Screenshot of the Reconciliation page header - [other artifacts as relevant] Thanks, NAME · CUSTOMER NAME

PO Receipts Reconciliation A walkthrough for reconciling the Received-Not-Vouchered (RNV) liability account using the RapidReconciler PO Receipts module. Use the sidebar to navigate, or follow the four-step process top to bottom. Getting Started If this is your first time, work through the topics in order using the sidebar — beginning with Purpose & Scope , then the Process Flow Diagram . Make sure to review Key Concepts before starting the four-step process — understanding the F43121 table and Match Types is essential to everything that follows. If you're looking up a specific step or reference, jump directly to it from the sidebar. The Four-Step Process Step Goal Where Step 1 Validate the Orders page — make sure the open receipts list is accurate before comparing to the GL Orders Page Step 2 Review the Reconciliation page — confirm the GL Balance, Open Receipts, and Out of Balance amounts Reconciliation Page Step 3 Work each unreconciled order using Line Analysis to find the offending document(s) Line Analysis Page Step 4 Post the adjusting JE and save audit documentation JD Edwards + Reconciliation Page Reference Sections Reference pages cover individual application surfaces and recurring concepts in depth. They are organized for lookup rather than sequential reading: Section What It Covers Orders Page Filters, suspensions, column definitions, line details, and validation checks Reconciliation Page Status indicators, account filters, Calculation Section fields, PO Receipts Aging Chart Line Analysis Page Edit Note button, Document button, filter drop-downs, Recalc and Export, Documents grid Suspension vs Exclusion When to suspend an entire order versus excluding a single document Common Issues Business-process and tooling issues with their resolutions Variance Patterns Quick-reference table of common variance patterns and their causes Recommended Cadence How often to perform each reconciliation activity Glossary Definitions for all acronyms and terms used throughout Key principle RapidReconciler does not correct RNV issues — all corrections are made in JD Edwards or through manual journal entries. RapidReconciler's role is to identify which purchase orders are unbalanced, at which line and document , and by how much — so the correct corrective action can be applied efficiently rather than discovered at period end.

Purpose & Scope The RapidReconciler PO Receipts module reconciles the Received-Not-Vouchered (RNV) balance sheet liability account against open purchase order receipt activity. The RNV account is controlled by DMAAI 4320 and holds the value of goods received from suppliers until the corresponding supplier invoice is matched and vouchered through Accounts Payable. Receipt table data ( F43121 ) is imported into RapidReconciler and balanced directly against general ledger details ( F0902 / F0911 ), eliminating the need for separate tools or manual matching processes. The module surfaces only the orders and documents that require attention — fully vouchered and reconciled orders are removed automatically. What is the RNV Account? The Received-Not-Vouchered (RNV) account is a temporary liability account that holds the value of goods received from a supplier until the corresponding supplier invoice is matched and vouchered. It acts as the bridge between the purchasing and accounts payable processes. At any point in time, the RNV account balance should equal the value of goods that have been received but not yet matched to a supplier invoice. If the balance does not agree to open receipt activity, a reconciling variance exists. The Standard Accounting Flow Step Transaction Account Debited Account Credited AAI 1 PO Receipt Inventory RNV 4310 / 4320 2 Voucher Match RNV A/P Trade 4320 / PC A balanced RNV account therefore shows offsetting debits and credits for every fully processed PO line, with a residual balance that reflects only goods received but not yet vouchered. Why RNV Reconciliation Is Challenging Without a dedicated tool, reconciling the RNV account requires manually matching F43121 records to general ledger entries — a time-consuming process that becomes increasingly complex as transaction volume grows. Common challenges include: Challenge Why It's Difficult Volume High-volume purchasing environments can have thousands of open receipts at any point in time. Aging receipts Old receipts that were never vouchered or were incorrectly closed accumulate over time and are difficult to identify manually. Manual vouchers Receipts cleared through manual journal entries rather than the standard voucher match process leave the F43121 record open, creating a false variance. Cost variances Price variances at voucher match (AAIs 4330, 4332, 4335) can leave residual balances in the RNV account that are difficult to trace. Currency differences In multi-currency environments, exchange rate variances at voucher match (AAI 4340) can create additional reconciling items. No As-Of reporting The F43121 table overwrites records on reversal rather than maintaining a transaction history, so reconciling to a prior point in time is not possible from the table itself. What RapidReconciler Reconciles Side Source Description Open Receipts Balance F43121 Match Type 1 records Receipts not yet vouchered GL Balance F0902 Account Balances General ledger balance for the RNV account Out of Balance Difference between the two What must be explained and resolved How RapidReconciler Helps Feature How It Helps Orders Page Automatically calculates the open receipt position for every purchase order and displays only those with open amounts or variances. Fully vouchered and reconciled orders are removed automatically. Reconciled / Suspended Filters Allow the user to focus on unreconciled orders, audit suspended orders, or validate the full open receipts listing — without manual sorting or filtering of raw data. Calculation Section Separates the total out-of-balance amount into Open Receipts, Unreconciled, and Batches components, and provides a Suggested Entry amount that excludes outstanding variances. PO Receipts Aging Chart Visual breakdown of open receipts by period — identifies whether the RNV balance is driven by recent activity or aging items. Suspension Feature Removes orders cleared by manual voucher entry, PRLAND = 3 landed cost records, or data cutoff artifacts from the variance calculation. Line Analysis Page Side-by-side F43121 vs. GL comparison at the document level, with the ability to exclude specific documents and add audit notes. Document Button Displays all transactions for a PO line with a Variance column identifying precisely which documents have discrepancies. Unreconciled Link Navigates directly from the Reconciliation page to a pre-filtered Orders page showing only the orders requiring action.

Process Flow Diagram The diagram below shows the end-to-end PO Receipts reconciliation flow at a glance. Each step is detailed in the four-step process pages. START Log in to RapidReconciler Navigate to PO Receipts module Confirm both status lights are green 1 Validate the Orders Page • Sort by MostRecReceipt asc — find aged receipts • Audit suspended orders — confirm each is still warranted AmtOpen total matches JD Edwards Open Receipts? NO Investigate timing / data differences YES 2 Review the Reconciliation Page • Confirm Batches = 0 • Validate GL Balance and Open Receipts • Identify Out of Balance and Unreconciled amounts Out of Balance = $0? YES NO 3 Work Unreconciled Orders • Click Unreconciled link → expand each order with [+] • Click Line Analysis on grey lines • Set Rec=All, Line=0, Query • Click Document button — find variances; correct or exclude; Recalc 4 Post Adjusting JE • All resolved → use Out of Balance amount • Carrying forward → use Suggested Entry amount END Save audit documentation The Four Steps at a Glance Step Goal Key Output 1 Validate the Orders page — make sure the open receipts list is accurate AmtOpen total agrees with JD Edwards Open Receipts report 2 Compare Open Receipts to the GL on the Reconciliation page Out of Balance amount and contributing variance sources 3 Work each unreconciled order using Line Analysis to find the offending document Variances corrected in JDE or excluded with notes; Out of Balance reduced 4 Post the adjusting journal entry and save audit documentation Out of Balance = $0 after next refresh; period closed

Before You Begin Confirm the prerequisites and operating rules before starting a reconciliation. Most surprises during the process come from skipping one of these checks. Prerequisites Item Requirement RapidReconciler access Login credentials provided by your administrator Module permissions Access to the PO Receipts module and applicable companies Suspension permission Must be granted by your administrator to suspend orders Exclusion permission Must be granted by your administrator to exclude documents JD Edwards access Required to investigate and correct issues identified in RapidReconciler Key Rules Before Starting Confirm before any reconciliation work Both status lights on the Reconciliation page must be green before making any adjustments to the general ledger. RapidReconciler data is refreshed nightly . Any JD Edwards report used for comparison should be run at approximately the same time as the RapidReconciler data was imported. The Open Receipts balance in RapidReconciler should match an Open Receipts report run directly from JD Edwards. Resolve any discrepancies between the two before making journal entries. The reconciliation objective is to ensure that amounts on receipts and vouchers match between F43121 and the GL — not to match individual vouchers to receipts. A receipt without a voucher is a legitimate open receipt. Reconciliation objective RapidReconciler reconciles the F43121 receipts table to the F0902 GL balance. The goal is not to match every individual voucher to its receipt — that's how JD Edwards processes them. The goal is to identify cases where the two data sources disagree , and to resolve those disagreements.

Key Concepts Reference material on the data model that powers the PO Receipts module. A working understanding of the F43121 table, Match Types, and the PRLAND field will save significant investigation time when variances appear. The F43121 Table The F43121 (PO Receiver) table is the source of truth for RNV reconciliation. It stores a record of every receipt and voucher match transaction on a purchase order line. RapidReconciler imports F43121 data and compares it directly to the corresponding GL entries to identify where the two do not agree. F43121 Match Types Each record in F43121 carries a Match Type field that identifies what stage of the purchasing process the record represents. Understanding match types is essential for interpreting the RNV reconciliation. Match Type Description Effect on RNV 1 Receipt — Created when goods are received against a purchase order (P4312). The initial record that opens the RNV balance. Opens RNV — credits the RNV account via AAI 4320 2 Voucher Match — Created when the supplier invoice is matched to the receipt (P4314 / P0411). Written when the Match Type 1 record transitions from open to paid. Closes RNV — debits the RNV account via AAI 4320 3 Reversal of Receipt — Created when a receipt is reversed. Offsets the original Match Type 1 record. Closes RNV for the reversed quantity 4 Reversal of Voucher Match — Created when a voucher match is reversed. Reopens RNV for the reversed amount 5 Landed Cost Receipt — Created when a landed cost is applied at the time of receipt (P4312). Opens RNV for the landed cost amount (AAI 4390) 6 Landed Cost Voucher Match — Created when a landed cost is matched to an invoice. Closes RNV for the landed cost amount Key point A fully reconciled purchase order line should have offsetting Match Type 1 and Match Type 2 records that net to zero. A line where Match Type 1 exists without a corresponding Match Type 2 represents an open receipt — a legitimate component of the RNV balance. Lines that appear unbalanced for reasons other than a missing voucher match are where reconciling variances originate. The PRLAND Field The PRLAND field in F43121 identifies whether a record is a standard receipt or a landed cost, and whether the landed cost is eligible for voucher match: PRLAND Value Meaning Blank Standard product/item receipt line 1 Product/item line 2 Landed cost — eligible for voucher match 3 Landed cost — accrual only, not eligible for voucher match PRLAND = 3 requires suspension When PRLAND = 3, the landed cost is accrued at receipt but will never be vouchered. These records contribute to the F43121 open balance but will never have a corresponding Match Type 2 record. They must be suspended in RapidReconciler to avoid creating a permanent false variance. No Period Selector — Why It Matters Unlike the Inventory and In Transit modules, the PO Receipts module has no period selector . Because the F43121 table overwrites records on reversal rather than creating new entries, As-Of reporting is not possible. The reconciliation is always performed as a current balance-to-balance comparison — open receipts now vs. GL balance now. This has two practical implications: The reconciliation must be performed regularly (weekly is recommended) so that issues are caught while the supporting evidence is still recent. JD Edwards reports used for comparison should be run at approximately the same time as the most recent RapidReconciler nightly import.

Step 1 Validate the Orders Page Before comparing RapidReconciler's open receipts total to the GL, validate that the Orders page itself is correct. The reconciliation is only as good as the underlying list — orders that appear here in error will produce phantom variances downstream. Login & Filters Log in to RapidReconciler at https://rapidreconciler.getgsi.com Navigate to the PO Receipts module Confirm both status lights at the top of the page are green — do not proceed if either is red Apply the appropriate company and account filters Navigate to the Orders page Figure 1 The PO Receipts Orders page with both status lights green and the standard validation filters applied (Reconciled = All; Suspended = No). Rows shaded gray are unreconciled (orders with non-zero variances); white rows are reconciled. Supplier names and the long account have been replaced with generic placeholders for documentation purposes. Validate the Orders List Set filters to Reconciled = All; Suspended = No Sort by MostRecReceipt ascending to identify the oldest open receipts. Rows shaded gray are unreconciled (have non-zero variances) and warrant the closest review; white rows are already reconciled Review every order on the list and decide its disposition (suspending an order requires permission granted by your administrator — see Before You Begin ): Order Type Action Genuinely in transit (recent receipt, voucher pending) Leave open — this is a legitimate component of the RNV balance Cleared by manual voucher (P0411 instead of P4314) Correct in JD Edwards if possible; otherwise suspend in RapidReconciler with a note PRLAND = 3 landed cost record (accrual only) Suspend — will never be vouchered PO at status 999 with no further activity possible Suspend with a note explaining the closure context Aged receipt under active investigation Suspend temporarily with a note describing the investigation Set filters to Reconciled = All; Suspended = Yes — confirm every suspended order still warrants suspension and has a note explaining why Once all suspensions and exclusions are complete, click Recalc in the top right of the page to refresh balances and variances. Recalc applies the changes you've just made without waiting for the nightly import — the recalc takes a few minutes, and a gear icon in the top right indicates progress. Confirm the AmtOpen total is accurate before proceeding Validate before comparing The open receipts listing must be validated before proceeding with the reconciliation. The quality of the reconciliation depends on the accuracy of the Orders page. Any order on the list in error — either correct in JD Edwards or suspend in RapidReconciler. Suspending Orders — the Edit Note Popup The disposition table above calls for suspending several order types. Suspension is applied through the Edit Note button at the top-left of the Orders grid, which opens the Suspended Note popup: Figure 2 The Suspended Note popup. One order (highlighted orange) is currently selected, but multi-select using standard Windows or macOS selection is supported. Supplier names behind the popup have been redacted. Select one or more rows in the grid using standard OS selection — click a row to select it, Ctrl-click (Windows) or Cmd-click (Mac) to add or remove individual rows from the selection, or Shift-click to select a range Click Edit Note at the top-left of the grid to open the popup Tick the Suspended checkbox to suspend the selected orders (or leave it unticked to add a note without changing suspension status) Type the note text describing why the orders are being suspended — be specific so the next reviewer (or auditor) can verify the disposition Click Save . The same note and suspension state are applied to every selected row Two different Edit Note popups RapidReconciler has two Edit Note buttons that open similar-looking popups but do different things. The popup shown here is on the Orders page and suspends entire orders. A different Edit Note button on the Line Analysis page excludes a single document from variance calculation — see Step 3 — The Edit Note Action for that workflow. Compare to JD Edwards With filters set to Reconciled = All; Suspended = No , the AmtOpen column total represents the open receipts position. Run an Open Receipts report directly from JD Edwards at approximately the same time as the most recent RapidReconciler nightly import, and compare: If the totals match — proceed to Step 2. If they don't — investigate timing differences first (the two reports may have been run at different points in time). If timing is not the cause, contact your administrator before continuing. Worked Example To make the validation procedure concrete, the table below summarizes a real Orders page export — 1,250 open receipts totaling $860,652.10 across two companies (10 and 50) and two currencies (USD and GBP). All rows are Suspended = No , so this is the “before” state — nothing has been triaged yet. Metric Value What it tells you Total open receipts $860,652.10 across 1,250 rows The candidate RNV balance — what will be compared to the GL once validated Rows with AmtOpen = 0 821 rows (66%) Receipts that are fully cleared on units but still appear because of small amount variances; sort by Variance to triage Rows with negative AmtOpen 7 rows (–$3,492.63) Over-vouchered or manually-vouchered receipts; classic candidates for the “P0411 instead of P4314” disposition Rows with non-zero Variance 812 rows ($93,124 absolute) Where reconciliation effort should be focused Most recent receipt date 2016-08-27 Sets the “as-of” date for comparison to the JD Edwards Open Receipts report Oldest receipt date 2015-09-01 ~12 months old at file basis — anything older than 90 days deserves a second look Three patterns the validator should investigate After sorting by MostRecReceipt ascending and scanning for the dispositions in the table above, three concrete cases stand out in this dataset: Case Evidence Likely Cause Disposition UoM mismatch on a single PO Order 281375: UnitsRec = 2,375 , UnitsVouch = 235 (10:1 ratio); Variance = $16,890 — the largest single variance in the file Receipt entered in eaches, voucher entered in tens (or similar UoM mismatch in the PO line) Correct in JD Edwards; if not feasible, suspend with note describing the UoM error Goods and freight vouchered against separate suppliers Orders 260823 and 265014 each show UnitsRec = 0 , AmtRec = $0 , UnitsVouch = 2,204 , AmtVouch = $5,092.68 , Variance = –$5,091.24 — identical amounts on two related supplier numbers (one for the goods supplier, one for the freight carrier) A landed-cost arrangement: the primary supplier is paid for the goods and the freight carrier is paid separately for shipping. Each voucher accrues as RNV but has no goods-receipt counterpart to clear it Suspend both rows with a note identifying them as freight / landed-cost accruals — they will not clear through normal voucher matching Aged receipts with no further activity ~90 rows have receipt dates before 2015-12-01 and UnitsOpen = 0 but small residual AmtOpen or Variance Penny-rounding or price-variance residuals on receipts that are otherwise fully matched; PO is likely at status 999 Review individually: penny variances under your materiality threshold can be suspended; investigate anything over the threshold before suspending What this validation should produce By the end of the procedure, every row in the export should be classified into one of five outcomes from the “Validate the Orders List” table above. In the example dataset, expect roughly 400–450 rows to remain Suspended = No as legitimate in-transit receipts; the rest should either be corrected in JD Edwards or moved to Suspended = Yes with a documented note. Only after that triage is the AmtOpen total ready to compare against the JD Edwards Open Receipts report.

Step 2 Review the Reconciliation Page With the Orders page validated, the Reconciliation page is where the open receipts balance is compared to the GL balance and all variance sources are identified. This is the page that tells you whether there is a problem and what kind of problem it is. Status Indicators Both status indicators at the top of the page must be green before making any adjustments to the general ledger: Indicator Green Means Red Means Action if Red PO Receipt Validation Carry-forward from prior period is accurate Prior period issue exists Hover for details; resolve before proceeding System Status JD Edwards import completed successfully Import error occurred Hover for details; contact your administrator Figure 3 The status bar at the top of the Reconciliation page. Both indicators are green, meaning the carry-forward from the prior period is accurate and the JD Edwards import completed successfully. Out of Balance — Skip Ahead? If Out of Balance = $0 , the period is reconciled — skip to Step 4 for audit documentation. If non-zero, work through the Calculation section below. Read the Calculation Section The Calculation section breaks the Out of Balance amount into its contributing parts: Field Required Before Close? Action GL Balance Informational Confirm matches the trial balance Open Receipts Validate first Confirm matches the JD Edwards Open Receipts report (Step 1) Out of Balance Resolve Investigate each contributing source Unreconciled Resolve or carry forward Click the link to navigate to unreconciled orders (Step 3) Batches Must be zero Work with finance to post outstanding GL batches Suggested Entry Use if carrying forward Journal entry amount excluding unreconciled orders Figure 4 The Calculation panel. Continuing the worked example from Step 1, the validated Open Receipts total of $860,652.10 is compared against the GL Balance of $836,158.60, producing an Out of Balance of –$24,493.50. The separate Unreconciled figure of $52,348.22 totals the variances on individual orders that don't yet reconcile internally — these are the orders to work through in Step 3. The Audit Report button at the bottom of the panel generates the period-end audit documentation once all variances have been resolved (see Step 4 ). Suggested Entry vs. Out of Balance Two amounts on this page are candidates for the closing journal entry. Which one to use depends on whether all unreconciled variances are resolved: Use Amount When to Use Out of Balance Includes unreconciled orders All unreconciled variances have been resolved before closing Suggested Entry Excludes unreconciled orders Unreconciled orders will be carried forward for resolution next period Best practice Resolve all unreconciled orders before closing the period. Using the Suggested Entry amount to close around outstanding variances defers the problem and accumulates carry-forward balances over time. Reach for Suggested Entry only when an unreconciled order genuinely cannot be addressed in the current cycle.

Step 3 Work Unreconciled Orders Step 3 is where the actual investigation happens. For each unreconciled order, the goal is to identify the specific document(s) where F43121 disagrees with the GL, determine the root cause, and either correct in JD Edwards or exclude the document with an audit note. Display Unreconciled Orders Click the Unreconciled link on the Reconciliation page. This opens the Orders page with filters preset to Reconciled = No; Suspended = No — showing only orders with variances. (Setting these filters manually on the Orders page produces the same result.) For Each Unreconciled Order Click the + icon on the left of the order row to expand it Identify which lines are displayed in grey — these are the unreconciled lines Click Line Analysis on the grey line. This navigates to the Line Analysis page with filter data pre-populated for that line. (See Line Analysis Page for full reference.) On the Line Analysis page, change the Rec filter to All and the Line filter to 0 , then click Query to display data for the entire order — useful when a variance spans multiple lines Click the Document button to view the F43121 vs. GL side-by-side comparison Review the Variance column — identify the specific document(s) showing a non-zero variance For each variant document, determine whether the discrepancy is in F43121, the GL, or both — see the Variance Patterns reference for common signatures Figure 5 An unreconciled order expanded with the + icon. The inner grid lists the individual receipt lines that make up the order, with a LINE ANALYSIS button on each line for navigating to that line's diagnostic page. Supplier names and the long/short account values have been replaced with generic placeholders for documentation purposes. Line Detail Columns Expanding an order or exporting from the expanded view (the ⬇ button in the top-right corner) reveals a richer set of columns than the parent Orders page. The fields specific to the line-detail view are: Column Meaning POLine Line number on the purchase order. Multiple lines can exist for one PO; gaps in the numbering typically indicate canceled or fully reconciled lines that no longer appear here NxtSts JD Edwards next status of the PO line. 400 indicates the line is open; 999 indicates the line is closed in JDE — closed lines can still carry residual variances that must be reconciled here even though no further activity is possible in JDE MT Match Type of the underlying F43121 record — see F43121 Match Types for the full list. The most common values on this view are 2 (Voucher Match) and 4 (Voucher Match Reversal); a Match Type 4 line often explains a re-opened line MostRecentRec Date of the most recent receipt activity for this specific line. Useful for spotting old activity that should have cleared ShortAccount Short-form account ID for the same RNV account shown in LongAccount on the parent row Item_Acct Item / inventory account for the line. In a single-RNV-account configuration this matches LongAccount , but they can differ in multi-account setups UnitsOpen / AmountOpen Units and dollars still open at the line level. A negative UnitsOpen indicates an over-vouchered line Variance Line-level variance. The sum of line variances on an order matches the order-level Variance on the parent Orders page Comment Free-text comment field — populated by Edit Note when a document on the line is excluded Worked Example — Reading the Lines Order 29442 from the Step 1 example expands into 8 receipt lines with the following totals and signals: Observation What it shows Line variances sum to $182.67 Matches the order-level Variance from the Step 1 worked example exactly — confirming the line-level data is the right place to investigate POLine numbers are 12, 13, 15, 16, 26, 27, 28, 29 Gaps (no line 14, 17–25) indicate other lines on the PO either reconciled cleanly or were canceled and aren't part of the open balance POLine 12 has UnitsOpen = –7.96 and MT = 4 Match Type 4 is a Voucher Match Reversal — this line was previously fully matched, but the voucher was reversed in JDE. The reversal re-opened the line and produced the largest individual variance among the open lines ($42.96) POLines 26 and 29 are at NxtSts = 999 These lines are closed in JDE (no further activity possible) but still carry residual variances of –$0.01 and $6.84. They must be addressed in RapidReconciler since JDE will not generate any further activity to clear them POLine 27 has UnitsOpen = 3.87 with AmountOpen = $334.36 but Variance = $19.96 The bulk of the open balance ($314.40) is a legitimate in-transit receipt awaiting voucher; only $19.96 is variance to investigate Where to focus When triaging a multi-line order, sort or scan by the Variance column rather than AmountOpen — a line can carry a large open amount that's simply awaiting voucher (legitimate RNV) while a different line with a tiny open amount holds the actual variance. In this order, POLine 16 ($85.74), POLine 12 ($42.96), and POLine 27 ($19.96) account for over 80% of the variance to resolve. Take Corrective Action Each variance has one of two outcomes: Correctable in JD Edwards Process the voucher match, reverse the bad transaction, or post a correcting journal entry directly in JDE After correction, the next nightly refresh will clear the variance from RapidReconciler. Known exception Suspend the order or exclude the specific document in RapidReconciler with an audit note See Suspension vs Exclusion for which to choose. Triage Aged Receipts in JDE — Open Receipts by Supplier When a variance traces back to a receipt that AP has not yet vouchered, point AP at the Open Receipts by Supplier view in JD Edwards. This is the same form ( W43214A , program P43214 ) that JDE techs use to reverse receipts, but its read-side view lists every open (un-vouchered) receipt for a supplier in one place — the right tool for clearing aged items before RapidReconciler's next nightly import. Menu path Receipts Matching and Posting ( G43A15 ) → Open Receipts by Supplier What AP does there Walks each open receipt by supplier and either creates the voucher, reverses the receipt, or posts a correcting entry When AP asks “I see the variance in RapidReconciler — how do I clear it in JDE?” the answer for aged open receipts is almost always: open this view, filter by the supplier, and work the list. Each line carries the receipt’s open quantity and open amount, so AP can see exactly which receipts still need vouchering, which were vouchered short, and which look stale enough to reverse. Reversal sequence reminder If a receipt on the list has been matched to a voucher and AP needs to back the whole thing out, JDE will not let them cancel the receipt directly. The voucher must be reversed first in P0411 (Match Voucher to Open Receipt), then the receipt reversed here in P43214 . See the reversal sequence note in the reference guide. The Edit Note Action When a document is being excluded, use the Edit Note button on the Line Analysis page. The Edit Note popup combines two actions in one place: Field Use Excluded checkbox Tick to remove the document's amount from the variance calculation after recalculation Note text box Audit text — describe why the exclusion is being made (mandatory in practice) If an exclusion is made in error, unchecking the box reinstates the document. After making any exclusions, click Recalc to refresh the variance calculation. The recalc takes a few minutes — a gear icon in the top right indicates progress. Helpful trick On the Line Analysis page, change the line number to 0 and click Query to display data for all lines on the PO. This is extremely useful when diagnosing variances that span multiple lines — the Document button will then show all documents for the entire order in one view.

section_title: Post the Adjusting JE & Save Audit Documentation
body: Step 4 Post the Adjusting JE & Save Audit Documentation Once all unreconciled orders have been addressed (corrected in JDE or excluded with notes), the period can be closed with a final journal entry and audit documentation produced. Choose the JE Amount Two amounts on the Reconciliation page are candidates for the closing journal entry. Which one to use depends on whether unreconciled variances were resolved or carried forward: If all variances resolved Use the Out of Balance amount Posting this entry should drive Out of Balance to $0 after the next nightly refresh. If carrying variances forward Use the Suggested Entry amount This excludes the unreconciled amount, which will reappear next period as Carry Forward. Common Offset Accounts for RNV Journal Entries The offset side of the closing JE depends on the nature of the variance being cleared: Situation Offset Account Unvouchered receipt to be written off Expense / Cost of Goods Sold Price variance between receipt and invoice Purchase Price Variance (AAI 4330) Goods consumed before voucher match Cost of Sales Variance (AAI 4332) Standard cost variance AAI 4335 Exchange rate variance (multi-currency) AAI 4340 Correction of a processing error Appropriate correcting account per the error Post the JE in JD Edwards Post the journal entry to the RNV account with the appropriate offset account. RapidReconciler will not see the JE until the next nightly refresh. Confirm Reconciliation Wait for the next nightly refresh — RapidReconciler does not see the JE until then Return to the Reconciliation page Confirm Out of Balance = $0 (or only the Carry Forward amount, if Suggested Entry was used) If a residual variance remains, return to Step 3 and identify which line moved Save Audit Documentation The PO Receipts module has a dedicated Audit Report button at the bottom of the Calculation panel on the Reconciliation page (see Step 2, Figure 4 ). Click it to generate a period-end audit report capturing the reconciled state — including the GL Balance, Open Receipts, Out of Balance, suspensions, and exclusions — and save the output to your audit folder for the period. When to run it Run the Audit Report only after every variance has been resolved (corrected in JDE, suspended with a note, or excluded with a note) and the closing journal entry has been posted. Running it earlier captures a still-in-progress state that won't match the final period-end balances.

Reference — The Orders Page The Orders page lists all purchase orders — summarized to the order level — where one or more of the following conditions exist: For screenshots of this page, see Figure 1 (default view), Figure 2 (Suspended Note popup), and Figure 5 (an order expanded to show line details). Open Receipts — A receipt document (Match Type 1) has not yet been matched to a supplier invoice (Match Type 2) Variances — A receipt or voucher in F43121 has an amount that does not match the corresponding GL entry in the RNV account Orders that have been fully received, fully vouchered, and where everything reconciles are automatically removed from the list. Filter Drop-Downs Filter All Yes No Reconciled Show all orders Show only reconciled orders Show only unreconciled orders Suspended Show all orders Show only suspended orders Show only non-suspended orders Recommended Filter Combinations Reconciled Suspended What You See Purpose All No All orders contributing to the Open Receipts balance Validate the total open receipts amount No No All unreconciled, non-suspended orders Identify orders requiring investigation All Yes All suspended orders Audit that suspensions are still warranted What Is a Suspension? A suspension in PO Receipts is equivalent to flagging an order as "Ignore Me." Suspending an order removes its amounts from the out-of-balance and variance calculations. Common Reasons to Suspend Reason Description Data cutoff artifact RapidReconciler was loaded by date and only the voucher was imported, not the original receipt. The order processed correctly — it's a start-up issue. Manual voucher clearance A supplier invoice was entered using standard voucher entry (P0411) instead of being matched to the PO (P4314), leaving a paid receipt showing as open in F43121. Correcting in JD Edwards is preferred; suspension is the alternative. PRLAND = 3 landed cost An accrual-only landed cost that will never be vouchered. Suspend to prevent a permanent false variance. Aged receipt under review An older open receipt being actively investigated. Suspend temporarily with a note explaining the status. PO at status 999 with no further activity possible Closed in JD Edwards but still showing residual F43121 balance. Suspension does not change JD Edwards Performing a suspension in RapidReconciler does not change any data in JD Edwards. If the order should be corrected in JD Edwards, make that correction first. If an order is suspended in error, it can be unsuspended. Suspension permission must be granted by your administrator. Best practice Document the reason for each suspension in the note field for audit purposes. Suspended orders should be reviewed periodically to confirm they remain appropriately suspended and have not had new activity applied against them. Orders Page Column Definitions Column Description Suspended Checkbox indicating the order has been suspended Note Icon indicating a text note has been entered. Click "Edit Note" to view. Company The company number associated with the RNV account Long Account The RNV account number being reconciled Supplier The address book number on the PO. May show two addresses if landed costs are applied. Name Name of the supplier on the row Order Purchase order number Type Purchase order type MostRecReceipt The date of the most recent receipt — key field for aging analysis UnitsRec Total units received AmtRec Total value of units received UnitsVouch Total units vouchered AmtVouch Total value of units vouchered UnitsOpen Units received but not yet vouchered AmtOpen Value of units received but not yet vouchered. When Reconciled = All and Suspended = No, this column total matches the Open Receipts balance on the Reconciliation page. RecTotal Total value of all order transactions from F43121 GLTotal Total value of all order transactions posted to the GL RNV account Variance RecTotal minus GLTotal. Ideally zero. Non-zero values indicate an issue to resolve. ExclAmount The total exclusion amount for the order CurrCode Currency code associated with amounts Key distinction AmtOpen reflects legitimate open receipts (receipts not yet vouchered). Variance reflects a discrepancy between F43121 and the GL for the same document — this is a reconciling issue, not a legitimate open receipt. Different columns, different actions. Line Details Click the + icon on the left of any order row to expand the line detail. This shows each individual PO line and whether it has an open receipt amount or a variance. Unreconciled lines are highlighted in grey Reconciled lines are displayed in white Details can be exported to Excel using the green down arrow Clicking the Line Analysis button within the expanded row navigates directly to the Line Analysis page with filter data pre-populated for that line. This is the fastest way to investigate a specific unreconciled line. Things to Check on the Orders Page Before proceeding to the Reconciliation page, validate the Orders page to ensure the Open Receipts balance is accurate. Work through the following checks: Check How What to Look For Identify aged open receipts Sort by MostRecReceipt ascending Oldest receipts at top — confirm whether they are still active or candidates for suspension or JD Edwards correction Audit suspended orders Set Reconciled = All; Suspended = Yes Confirm every suspended order still genuinely warrants suspension — review the note for each Identify orders requiring review Set Reconciled = No; Suspended = No These orders have variances that must be resolved Review unreconciled lines Expand any order by clicking + Grey lines indicate unreconciled lines — click Line Analysis for detail Validate the open receipts total Set Reconciled = All; Suspended = No The AmtOpen total must be validated before comparing to the GL balance

Reference — The Reconciliation Page The Reconciliation page is the default page displayed on login. This is where the open receipts balance is compared to the GL balance and all variance sources are identified. For screenshots of this page, see Figure 3 (status indicators) and Figure 4 (Calculation panel). Status Indicators Both indicators must be green before making any adjustments to the general ledger. Indicator Green Means Red Means Action if Red PO Receipt Validation Carry-forward from prior period is accurate Prior period issue exists Hover for details; resolve before proceeding System Status JD Edwards import completed successfully Import error occurred Hover for details; contact your administrator Account Filters Operate as a hierarchy from left to right ( Company → Business Unit → Object → Subsidiary ) Removing a company automatically removes associated business units, objects, and subsidiaries Search rows at the top of each column filter within that column Filter selections persist across pages within the PO Receipts module Calculation Section Field Description Source GL Balance General ledger balance for the RNV account F0902 — matches the trial balance exactly Open Receipts Calculated from F43121 Match Type 1 records F43121 Out of Balance Difference between GL Balance and Open Receipts Zero = fully reconciled Unreconciled Total variance between F43121 and GL for unreconciled orders Orders page Variance column total Batches GL batches awaiting posting Unposted F0911 entries Total Variance Sum of Unreconciled and Batches amounts — Suggested Entry Out of Balance minus Unreconciled amount The journal entry amount if unreconciled orders are excluded from the current close Manual Entries Manual adjustments made to the selected accounts Informational Suggested Entry vs. Out of Balance Use Amount When to Use Out of Balance Includes unreconciled orders Use when all unreconciled variances have been resolved before closing Suggested Entry Excludes unreconciled orders Use when unreconciled orders will be carried forward for resolution next period Best practice Resolve all unreconciled orders before closing the period. Using the Suggested Entry amount to close around outstanding variances defers the problem and accumulates carry-forward balances over time. PO Receipts Aging Chart The aging chart provides a visual breakdown of open receipts by period. Use this to identify whether open receipts are recent (normal) or aging (requires investigation). A large proportion of aged open receipts is a signal that the voucher match process is not keeping pace with receipts, and may indicate: Supplier invoices that were never received or processed Purchase orders that were incorrectly closed without a voucher match Disputed invoices being held outside the normal process System errors that prevented the voucher match from completing Addressing aging RNV items regularly prevents the balance from accumulating to a point where it becomes difficult to manage.

Reference — The Line Analysis Page The Line Analysis page is the diagnosis surface — where individual receipt and voucher documents are compared between F43121 and the GL. This is where most reconciliation work happens during Step 3. Accessing the Line Analysis Page Always access from the Orders page Always access the Line Analysis page by clicking the Line Analysis button on an expanded order row on the Orders page. This pre-populates the filter with the correct order and line data. Clicking the Line Analysis link in the Main Navigation pane does not populate filter data and may cause confusion. Edit Note Button (Notes and Exclusions) The Edit Note button is used to: Add a note for future reference or audit documentation Exclude a specific document (receipt or voucher) from variance calculations Procedure Click the grid row(s) to be edited Type in the text box to add a note Check the Excluded box to remove amounts from the variance calculation after recalculation Click Save If an exclusion is made in error, unchecking the box will reinstate the document. Exclusion permission must be granted by your administrator. When to exclude a document Document exclusion at the line level is different from order suspension. Use document exclusion when a specific receipt or voucher document has a known discrepancy that is being tracked — for example, a receipt that was partially reversed out of sequence, creating an entry in F43121 that no longer matches the GL. See Suspension vs Exclusion for which to use. Document Button The Document button displays a pop-up listing all documents associated with the PO line, combining data from F43121 and F0911. This provides a side-by-side comparison of the receipts table and GL amounts for each document. Column Description Cutoff Date The earliest date considered during import. Discrepancies caused by the cutoff date are less likely the further the capture date is from the cutoff. Capture Date The date the data was imported into RapidReconciler Variance The difference between the F43121 amount and the GL amount by document — the key field to review Filter Drop-Downs Filter Options Use Exc (Excluded Docs) All / Yes / No Show all, only excluded, or only non-excluded documents Rec (Reconciled Docs) All / Yes / No Show all, only reconciled, or only unreconciled documents Doc Individual document selection Filter to a specific document number Helpful trick Change the line number to 0 and click Query to display data for all lines on the PO. This is extremely useful when diagnosing variances that span multiple lines. The Document button will then show all documents for the entire order. Recalc and Export Recalc — Recalculates variances after documents have been excluded. A gear icon in the top right corner indicates the recalculation is in progress. Results are available within a few minutes. Export — Exports grid data to Excel. Documents Grid Lists all transactions for the PO line(s) being filtered, split by data source: F43121 rows — Receipt and voucher data from the receipts table F0911 rows — GL detail entries for the same transactions Reconciled rows are displayed in light color ; unreconciled rows are displayed in grey For a side-by-side comparison, click the Document button rather than reading the grid rows individually.

Reference — Suspension vs. Exclusion A common point of confusion is when to suspend an order versus when to exclude a document . Both remove amounts from variance calculations, but they operate at different levels. For a screenshot of the suspension dialog (the Suspended Note popup), see Figure 2 . Action Scope Use When Order Suspension Entire purchase order The whole order should be ignored — e.g., PRLAND = 3 landed cost, manual voucher clearance for the entire order, or a data cutoff artifact affecting the whole order Document Exclusion Individual document (single receipt or voucher) The order is mostly correct, but a specific document has a known discrepancy — e.g., a single receipt reversed out of sequence, a tax rounding difference, or a manual GL entry tied to one document Rule of thumb If the issue affects every line on the order — suspend the order . If the issue is limited to a specific receipt or voucher number — exclude the document . Where Each Action Lives Action Where How Order Suspension Orders page Tick the Suspended checkbox on the order row Document Exclusion Line Analysis page Click Edit Note on a document row, tick the Excluded box, save, then click Recalc Neither changes JD Edwards Both actions affect only RapidReconciler — neither changes any data in JD Edwards. Always document the reason in the note field. Always add a note Whether suspending or excluding, always populate the note field with a clear explanation of why. The notes form the audit trail and remind future reviewers (including yourself) why an action was taken.

Reference — Common Issues The most frequently encountered RNV reconciliation issues — both at the underlying business-process level and at the RapidReconciler tooling level. Business-Process Issues Issue Cause Resolution Unmatched receipt Goods received but supplier invoice not yet processed Process voucher match in JD Edwards (P4314) when the invoice is received Manual GL entry used to clear RNV A journal entry was posted directly to the RNV account instead of processing through voucher match Suspend the order in RapidReconciler; verify the GL is correct Price variance at voucher match Invoice amount differs from receipt amount; AAI 4330 or 4332 not configured to clear RNV Review AAI configuration; post manual journal entry if needed Standard cost variance Receiving at a different cost than the standard (AAI 4335) Verify DMAAI 4335 configuration for the applicable order type Exchange rate variance Multi-currency orders where the rate changed between receipt and voucher match Review AAI 4340; post manual entry to clear residual Landed cost not vouchered Landed cost applied at receipt with PRLAND = 2 but voucher match not completed Process voucher match for the landed cost; or set PRLAND = 3 if accrual only Aging open receipts Old receipts never vouchered accumulating in the balance In JDE, open Open Receipts by Supplier ( G43A15 → P43214 ) to walk each aged item by supplier; process voucher match, reversal, or manual entry as appropriate Duplicate receipt or voucher Same receipt or voucher processed more than once Reverse the incorrect transaction in JD Edwards Purchase order incorrectly closed Order set to status 999 without voucher match Reopen and reprocess through standard voucher match, or write off via journal entry RapidReconciler Tooling Issues Issue Cause Resolution Open Receipts balance does not match JD Edwards Timing difference — RapidReconciler imports nightly; JD Edwards report run at a different time Run both reports at approximately the same time; confirm timing of last import Suspended order reappears after unsuspension New activity posted against the order Review the new activity; re-suspend if appropriate or process the voucher match Aged open receipts accumulating Supplier invoices not being processed promptly; or receipts processed without corresponding voucher matches Investigate each aged receipt; contact the supplier for the invoice; consider writing off if the receipt is no longer valid Large number of unreconciled orders DMAAI misconfiguration causing receipts to post to a different account than expected Review DMAAI 4320 configuration Variance on a specific document Reversed receipt or voucher processed out of sequence; rounding difference; tax entry mismatch Use Line Analysis Document view to compare F43121 vs. GL; exclude the document if the discrepancy is known and justified PRLAND = 3 records causing permanent variance Accrual-only landed costs that will never be vouchered Suspend the order in RapidReconciler Tax entries creating unexpected variances Tax explanation codes S, U, V, C, or B generate different AAI entries at receipt vs. voucher match Review tax configuration Carry-forward balance growing each period Unresolved variances being deferred using Suggested Entry instead of being resolved Prioritize working unreconciled orders before close; avoid using Suggested Entry as a routine practice Status light is red Prior period issue or import failure Hover for details; contact administrator if import failed Batches amount is non-zero Unposted F0911 batches Work with finance to post outstanding batches before closing

Reference — Variance Patterns Quick-reference table for the most common variance patterns seen on the Line Analysis Documents grid. Match the pattern to identify the likely cause and the appropriate corrective action. Variance Pattern Likely Cause Corrective Action F43121 amount differs from GL Receipt cost and GL entry do not match Investigate the receipt transaction; post a manual journal entry to the GL if needed GL entry exists but no F43121 record Manual GL entry posted to RNV account Exclude the GL document in Line Analysis; document the manual entry F43121 record exists but no GL entry Unposted batch Post the outstanding batch in JD Edwards Reversal out of sequence Receipt or voucher reversed after period close Investigate the reversal; post a correcting journal entry Cutoff date artifact Data imported from a date after the original receipt Exclude the document if the original receipt predates the import cutoff How to use this table On the Line Analysis page, click the Document button and review the Variance column. Each non-zero variance will fit one of the patterns above. Identify which side (F43121 or GL) contains the unexpected value, then follow the corresponding corrective action.

Reference — Recommended Cadence RapidReconciler's data refreshes nightly and the F43121 table has no As-Of capability — the reconciliation should be performed on a regular schedule rather than only at period end. Activity Recommended Frequency Review Orders page for aged receipts Weekly Audit suspended orders Monthly Full reconciliation review Weekly Period-end closing activities Period-end close Why Weekly The F43121 table overwrites records on reversal rather than maintaining a transaction history. This means reconciling to a prior point in time is not possible — once a record changes, the original state is gone. Performing the reconciliation weekly ensures that: Issues are identified while the supporting evidence is still recent Aging receipts don't accumulate to the point of being difficult to investigate Carry-forward balances don't snowball across periods Key Benefits Recap Benefit What It Means Consolidated platform RNV reconciliation is performed in the same application as inventory and goods in-transit reconciliation, reducing the need for multiple tools Automated balancing Receipt table data is automatically imported and compared to general ledger details, eliminating manual matching Focused exception reporting The Unreconciled link surfaces only the orders with issues, saving time during the reconciliation process Flexible handling Orders cleared by manual voucher can be suspended, and individual documents can be excluded, preventing them from distorting variance calculations Full drill-down capability From the summary level down to the individual document, all detail is accessible within the application Aging visibility Open receipts can be reviewed by age to identify and address items that have been outstanding for extended periods Audit trail Notes can be attached to suspensions and exclusions, providing documentation for audit review

Reference — Glossary Definitions for acronyms, JD Edwards programs, and table names referenced throughout this walkthrough. Term Definition AAI Automatic Accounting Instruction — JD Edwards configuration that maps transactions to GL accounts DMAAI Distribution/Manufacturing AAI — the AAI subset used by purchasing and inventory transactions F0902 Account Balances table — source of GL balance F0911 Account Ledger table — source of GL transaction detail F43121 PO Receiver table — source of receipt and voucher match transaction detail Match Type Field in F43121 identifying the type of transaction (1 = Receipt, 2 = Voucher Match, 3 = Reversal of Receipt, 4 = Reversal of Voucher Match, 5 = Landed Cost Receipt, 6 = Landed Cost Voucher Match) P4312 JD Edwards PO Receipts program P4314 JD Edwards Voucher Match program P0411 JD Edwards Standard Voucher Entry program PRLAND F43121 field identifying record as standard receipt or landed cost RNV Received-Not-Vouchered — the temporary liability account holding the value of received but unvouchered goods Suspension RapidReconciler action that flags an order to be ignored in variance calculations Exclusion RapidReconciler action that removes a specific document from variance calculations

page_title: JD Edwards Purchase Order Comprehensive Reference Guide
body: Glossary and Key Concepts Term Definition PO Receipts / RNV (Received-Not-Vouchered) A balance sheet liability for goods or services received against a purchase order where the supplier invoice has not yet been processed. Controlled by DMAAI 4320. Open PO A purchase order or line where not all items have been received. No receipt exists; no accounting entries; no RNV balance. 2-Way Match Receipt and voucher are processed simultaneously; RNV is bypassed entirely. 3-Way Match Standard process: PO → Receipt → Voucher Match. RNV is opened at receipt and closed at voucher match. F43121 (PO Receiver) The source-of-truth table for all PO receipts and voucher matches. Not a true ledger — reversals overwrite records rather than appending new ones. F0911 / F0902 General ledger transaction detail (F0911) and period-end balances (F0902). Used as the GL side of the RNV reconciliation. Match Type Field on F43121 that identifies what stage of the purchasing process each record represents (1 = receipt, 2 = voucher match, 3 = receipt reversal, 4 = voucher reversal, 5/6 = landed cost equivalents). Doc Type: OP / OV / PV The procurement cycle uses three distinct doc-type codes, and confusing them is a common source of misreading a transaction. OP = purchase-order header (no accounting). OV = PO Receipt event (P4312) — debits inventory via AAI 4310, credits RNV via AAI 4320. PV = Voucher Match event (P4314) — debits RNV via AAI 4320 to close it, credits A/P (PCAAI). Variances on a PV voucher route through AAI 4330. OV is NOT a voucher; it’s the inventory-creating receipt half of the cycle. DMAAI Distribution/Manufacturing Automatic Accounting Instructions. Numeric AAIs (e.g., 4310, 4320) that direct procurement transactions to the correct GL accounts. PRLAND F43121 field indicating whether a record is a standard receipt or a landed cost, and if landed, whether it is eligible for voucher match. PRLVLA F43121 field storing the Landed Cost Level — a three-character code from UDC 40/CA. Landed Cost Additional fees added to an item's cost (harbor fees, brokerage, duties, commissions) above the purchase price. Receipts Routing A configurable series of inspection and handling steps between supplier receipt and entry into usable stock. Critical distinction: "Open PO" and "Open Receipt" are not the same thing. Open POs create no accounting entries. Open Receipts (Match Type 1 with no offsetting Match Type 2) are the legitimate component of the RNV balance.

page_title: JD Edwards Purchase Order Comprehensive Reference Guide
section_title: PO Receipts and the RNV Account
body: PO Receipts and the RNV Account 2.1 Definition PO Receipts, also known as Received-Not-Vouchered (RNV) , is a balance sheet liability that accounts for goods and services received against a purchase order where the supplier invoice has not yet been processed. The RNV account is controlled by DMAAI 4320 in JD Edwards. It is credited when goods are received (the liability is recognized) and debited when the voucher is matched (the liability is cleared). 2.2 Why the RNV Account Matters The RNV account is a temporary liability. It should be self-clearing over time as supplier invoices are received and vouchered. A large or growing RNV balance typically indicates one or more of the following: Supplier invoices are not being processed promptly Receipts are being processed incorrectly or out of sequence Voucher matches are being entered manually rather than through the standard process Aged open receipts are accumulating without investigation Permanent open balances exist from PRLAND = 3 landed costs that have not been suspended 2.3 Healthy vs. Unhealthy RNV Balance Indicators Indicator Healthy Unhealthy Balance trend Stable or fluctuating with normal activity Growing month over month Receipt aging Most items < 60 days old Significant balance in items > 90 days old Manual GL entries Rare and well-documented Frequent direct journal entries to RNV Reconciling variance Near zero or fully explained Persistent unexplained variance PRLAND = 3 records Identified and suspended Mixed in with active receipts

page_title: JD Edwards Purchase Order Comprehensive Reference Guide
body: Process Flows (2-Way and 3-Way Match) 3.1 Standard 3-Way Match The standard PO Receipts process follows these steps: Step Event F43121 Record GL Impact 1 Purchase order created No record None 2 Goods received (P4312) Match Type 1 created Inventory/Expense debit ; RNV credit 3 Supplier invoice received — — 4 Voucher match processed (P4314) Match Type 2 created RNV debit ; A/P Trade credit 5 Payment issued — A/P Trade debit ; Cash/Bank credit At Step 4, the RNV account is fully cleared and the liability transfers to Accounts Payable. The PO Receipts balance for this transaction returns to zero. 3.2 2-Way Match In a 2-way match, the receipt and voucher are processed simultaneously. No separate receipt step occurs: Step Event F43121 Record GL Impact 1 Purchase order created No record None 2 Receive and voucher simultaneously (P4314) Match Type 1 and 2 created together Non-stock account debit ; A/P Trade credit 3 Payment issued — A/P Trade debit ; Cash/Bank credit Note: In a 2-way match, the temporary liability AAIs 4320 and 4355 are not used. The RNV account is bypassed entirely. 3.3 Reversals Reversals are a critical factor in understanding F43121 behavior. When a receipt or voucher match is reversed in JD Edwards, the original F43121 record is overwritten rather than a new record being created: Reversal Type F43121 Behavior Match Type Change Receipt reversal Original Match Type 1 record is overwritten Match Type changes from 1 to 3 Voucher match reversal Original Match Type 2 record is overwritten Match Type changes from 2 to 4 This overwrite behavior is the primary reason F43121 does not function as a true ledger and why As-Of reporting is not available for RNV. Reversal Sequence: Voucher Before Receipt When a receipt has already been matched to a voucher, JD Edwards will not let you cancel the receipt directly. The Procurement guide states: “you cannot cancel a receipt record. Instead, you must reverse the voucher in Match Voucher to Open Receipt” and then reverse the receipt. The canonical sequence is therefore: Reverse the voucher in P0411 (Match Voucher to Open Receipt) — this writes the Match Type 4 record. Reverse the receipt in P43214 (Open Receipts by Supplier) — this writes the Match Type 3 record. Important: Reversing out of order is the most common cause of orphaned F43121 / F43099 entries and stranded RNV balances. If a user reverses the receipt first while a matched voucher still points at it, JD Edwards either blocks the cancel or leaves F43121 in an inconsistent state that no longer ties back to the A/P ledger. This is the root cause of many of the “out-of-sequence” reconciliation states RapidReconciler surfaces — voucher reversal was skipped, was processed after the receipt reversal, or hit a different period.

page_title: JD Edwards Purchase Order Comprehensive Reference Guide
body: The F43121 Table 4.1 Overview Table F43121 (PO Receiver) is the source of truth for purchase order receipts. It stores a record for every receipt and voucher match transaction on a purchase order line. Critical behavior: F43121 does not behave as a standard ledger. During a receipt or voucher reversal, the original record is overwritten with new information rather than a new record being created.
